book_claims.claim: Listed as a plant milk providing vitamin D (101 IU per 8 fl oz unsweetened, 25.25% DV) when fortified
book_claims.mechanism: Fortified plant milk serves as a vitamin D source for those avoiding dairy

supplementary_research.finding: Rice milk has the highest arsenic content among plant milks; daily large-volume consumption not recommended especially in children; adults with varied diet face lower risk
Fortified plant milks including rice milk can effectively deliver vitamin D and calcium; bioavailability comparable to dairy milk when properly fortified

nutrient_highlights.bioavailability_notes: Only present when fortified; equivalent to dairy milk fortification
Higher carbohydrate than other plant milks; high glycemic index; consider for those with blood sugar concerns
When fortified; bioavailability ~30%, similar to dairy milk

synergies.description: Fortified rice milk is a dairy-free vehicle for vitamin D; aligns with book's recommendation for plant-based milk alternatives
Both plant milks serve as dairy replacements; almond milk has lower carbs, rice milk has lower allergen profile
Traditional breakfast pairing; rice milk adds carbohydrates alongside oat's fiber and beta-glucan
